status: Private preview with continuous premium-improvement loop
summary: Lang5K is now hidden behind a preview login and has a simple owner panel. The product is stronger than before, but still not at the finished premium bar. The main remaining gaps are synced accounts, a real member system, deeper course curation, and stronger automation around retention and operations.
doneNow: Protected the site behind a private preview login.
Added a simple admin page with a readable owner report.
Started a hidden PC loop that keeps pushing Lang5K toward premium and waits 15 minutes after each finished run before starting again.
Built a coached first-session path with a real Today's plan card.
Added same-session repair so Again and Hard cards return later in the same study session.
Unified guided study, cloze, and dictation around Again / Hard / Good / Easy.
canDoNext: Add passwordless accounts and synced progress so learners are no longer tied to one browser.
Create a proper member model before pricing is finalized.
Build stronger course-quality QA for the first 1,000 Russian sentences.
Add event tracking for lesson starts, completions, weak-item repair, and return behavior.
Add reminder automation and a more detailed owner status page.
needsYou: Approve cloud accounts and stored learner progress as part of the product.
Approve the default premium tone: practical and neutral early, heavier/dramatic content later.
Later, approve the real yearly subscription rollout and final pricing.
biggestBlockers: No real accounts or synced progress yet.
Still using access-token style gating instead of a real member lifecycle.
Russian course is improved but not fully premium-curated yet.
No reminders, analytics dashboard, or operational monitoring yet.
suggestedNow: Finish private preview mode cleanly, then keep the site hidden until the premium bar is genuinely met.
Build accounts plus synced progress before returning to pricing.
Tighten the first 1,000-sentence path so it feels premium from day one.
